title: GH #27.2: attempt-ledger RMW under store lock
action: Per D-GHF-C: wrap the read-mutate-write bodies of recordVerify (~:650), blockCell (~:835), capCell (~:700) and resetCellBudget (~:1221) in withStoreLock(root, 'cells:<id>', ...) following the exact pattern state.mjs:1649 / reservations.mjs:136 use (import from lock.mjs). Readers stay lock-free. Add a test (test_store_lock.mjs or test_claim_race.mjs, wherever the harness fits): two concurrent recordVerify calls on the same cell both land in trace.attempts (no lost entry). Run onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply. Commit: fix(cells): ledger writes under store lock (GH #27) [ghf-4].

truths: concurrent verify/block/cap never drops a ledger entry
lock key is per-cell so distinct cells never serialize
prohibitions: no nested withStoreLock deadlock (check callers that already hold a lock)

verify: node scripts/test_store_lock.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs

trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/lock.mjs (withStoreLock API)",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/reservations.mjs:136 (reserve() lock-wrapped usage precedent)",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs:1626-1649 (startFeature's D6 async-ification precedent — same shape, same test_lib.mjs await-cascade already done once for this codebase)",
    "scripts/test_claim_race.mjs (child-process racer harness pattern reused for the new scenarios)",
    "scripts/test_store_lock.mjs (fault-injection/widened-window pattern for a deterministic negative control)"
  ],
  "tests_added": [
    "scripts/test_claim_race.mjs scenario (e): 8 real recordVerify() racers (actual production function, no widening) on one cell -> asserts trace.attempts.length === 8 and unique n values (zero lost/clobbered entries)",
    "scripts/test_claim_race.mjs scenario (f): deliberate-red test-owned proxy mimicking recordVerify's PRE-FIX unlocked read-mutate-write shape with a widened window -> deterministic negative control proving the loss class is real"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "RED, captured against the unfixed skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/cells.mjs (recordVerify/capCell/blockCell/resetCellBudget were plain synchronous read-cell -> mutate-trace -> writeCell bodies with NO lock around them). Ran the new scenario (e) — 8 real concurrent recordVerify() calls on one cell via child-process racers (node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs) — and it failed: `(e) expected exactly 8 trace.attempts entries (one per racer, zero lost), got 4: [{\"n\":1,...},{\"n\":2,...},{\"n\":3,...},{\"n\":4,...}]`. Only 4 of 8 concurrent verify calls survived; the other 4 were silently dropped because two racers' read-mutate-write cycles interleaved and the later writeCell() clobbered the earlier one's appended trace.attempts entry — exactly GH #27's reported ledger-loss hazard, reproduced with the real production function under real OS-process concurrency, not a simulated proxy.",
  "verification_run": "node scripts/test_store_lock.mjs && node scripts/test_claim_race.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs — all green post-fix. scenario (e) now reports \"8 real recordVerify racers on one cell -> zero lost trace.attempts entries\"; scenario (f)'s deliberate-red proxy still demonstrates loss (detector bites, falsifiability preserved). test_lib.mjs: 400 passed, 0 failed (recordVerify/capCell/blockCell/resetCellBudget becoming async cascaded to ~79 direct call sites + 17 assertThrows->assertRejects conversions + 2 local test helpers made async, all mechanical, no assertion logic changed). test_heartbeat_touch.mjs: 29 rows ALL PASS. test_bee_cli.mjs (CLI-level, exercises the now-async bee.mjs handlers through the real dispatcher which already awaits every handler): 210 passed, 0 failed."
}
